deltas.releaseNoteText: | `reviewerEmails` | string[]  | Optional. 0–50 reviewer email addresses. Surfaced in the human step's `output.reviewerEmails` for downstream notification UIs.
deltas.summary: Add reviewerEmails (string[], 0–50 addresses, optional) to the human node config shape; it surfaces in the step's output.reviewerEmails after resume.

parameters.name: reviewerEmails
parameters.description: 0–50 reviewer email addresses. Surfaced in the human step's output.reviewerEmails for downstream notification UIs.

deltas.releaseNoteText: | `commentBody` | string    | `≤ 8000` chars. Stored on the human step's output for use by your reviewer-facing UI. The engine does not auto-create a Velt annotation per human step in v1 — your application is responsible for surfacing this string to reviewers (and, if you use the legacy comment-resolution flow, for creating the comment thread the reviewer replies to).
deltas.summary: Clarify that commentBody is stored in the step output only — the engine does NOT auto-create a Velt annotation or comment thread per human step; the application is responsible for surfacing it to reviewers.

Document the onReject shorthand on human node config: Form A (routeToNodeId synthesizes a reject-gated edge) and Form B (loopBack synthesizes a loop region). Every human node must now satisfy strict-mode: either config.onReject is set OR the node is a body member of a top-level loops[] entry — otherwise INVALID_ARGUMENT. Desugaring strips onReject from config and appends synthesized edges/loops to top-level arrays; GET /definitions/get returns canonical form. Warn that onReject.routeToNodeId on a joinOnQuorum group member is dead code at runtime.

parameters.name: onReject.routeToNodeId
parameters.description: Synthesizes a reject-gated edge from this node to routeToNodeId.

parameters.name: onReject.loopBack.toNodeId
parameters.description: Synthesizes a top-level loop region with entryNodeId = toNodeId.

parameters.name: onReject.loopBack.onExhausted.routeToNodeId
parameters.description: Node spawned when maxIterations is reached.

deltas.releaseNoteText:
## Loop regions

A loop region lets a workflow re-enter an earlier node when a reviewer rejects, instead of failing outright. Declare loops at the top level of a definition, peer to `groups[]`.
deltas.summary: Add a new Loop Regions section documenting the loops[] top-level definition field: fields (loopId, entryNodeId, bodyNodeIds, onIterationReject.when, onExhausted.routeToNodeId, maxIterations 1–20), body-shape constraints (single-terminal sequential OR group-bounded with joinOnQuorum + quorum===expectedSteps), previousAttempts payload threaded into iteration N+1, and iteration-scoped parallel group behavior. When to use a loop vs. the onReject shorthand: use a loop for multiple parallel reviewers sharing a retry counter or for explicit loopId control.

parameters.name: entryNodeId
parameters.description: Node spawned first on each iteration. Must be in bodyNodeIds.

parameters.name: onIterationReject.when
parameters.description: Predicate to trigger iteration N+1. Default: decision == reject && rejectorMandatory == true.

parameters.name: onExhausted.routeToNodeId
parameters.description: Node spawned when maxIterations reached. Omitting it causes execution to fail.

deltas.releaseNoteText: | `loop-duplicate-id` ... | `loop-group-bounded-quorum-must-equal-expected` | (9 new linter codes in the linter table)
deltas.summary: Append 9 new loop-related linter codes to the linter reference section (was 16 rules, now 25): loop-duplicate-id, loop-entry-must-be-in-body, loop-body-member-missing, loop-body-unreachable-from-entry, loop-body-must-have-single-terminal, loop-node-in-multiple-loops, loop-on-exhausted-route-to-not-found, loop-on-exhausted-route-to-in-body, loop-group-bounded-quorum-must-equal-expected.

details.codeExample:
loop-duplicate-id                         Two loops share the same loopId.
loop-entry-must-be-in-body                entryNodeId is not listed in bodyNodeIds.
loop-body-member-missing                  A bodyNodeIds entry isn't a declared node.
loop-body-unreachable-from-entry          Some body node is unreachable from entryNodeId along body-internal edges.
loop-body-must-have-single-terminal       Body shape is neither single-terminal sequential nor group-bounded.
loop-node-in-multiple-loops               A node appears in more than one loop body.
loop-on-exhausted-route-to-not-found      onExhausted.routeToNodeId references an unknown node.
loop-on-exhausted-route-to-in-body        onExhausted.routeToNodeId is itself a body node — must escape the loop.
loop-group-bounded-quorum-must-equal-expected  Group-bounded body joinOnQuorum group requires quorum === expectedSteps.

deltas.releaseNoteText:
| `loop.iteration-started` | same | Iteration N+1 spawns after a body iteration terminated rejected and the cap wasn't hit. | `{ loopId, iteration, triggeredBy: 'rejection' }` |
| `loop.exhausted` | same | The loop's `maxIterations` cap was reached. | `{ loopId, iteration, lastRejectedBy?, lastRejectionReason? }` |
deltas.summary: Add two new event types to the webhook event catalog: loop.iteration-started (fires when iteration N+1 spawns after a rejection; data: { loopId, iteration, triggeredBy: 'rejection' }) and loop.exhausted (fires when maxIterations reached; data: { loopId, iteration, lastRejectedBy?, lastRejectionReason? }).
